reasoning: The FRED trend analysis provides a P(increase) = 0.30 for a ~1-week horizon (by Nov 16), which acts as the primary anchor. I start slightly above at 0.32 because the 30y yield has trended positively over the past two weeks (4.55 to 4.70), showing upward momentum despite the overarching 6-week negative slope. For the 1-month (Dec 9, 2025) and 3-month (Feb 7, 2026) horizons, as instructed, uncertainty increases and pushes the probabilities closer toward 0.50 (0.41 and 0.47, respectively). The market context—elevated long yields and steepening curve expectations—supports keeping estimates balanced over longer horizons rather than purely extrapolating a short-term downward drift.
